articles.title: Hormuz reopening, end of blockade, Lebanon ceasefire: What's in the 14-point US-Iran peace deal
articles.description: A draft memorandum between the US and Iran reportedly includes an immediate end to military operations, lifting of sanctions on oil exports, and a $300 billion reconstruction package. The agreement, expected to be signed June 19, focuses on ending hostilities and paving the way for future nuclear talks.

articles.title: Noida Int'l Airport begins commercial operations; first IndiGo flight lands at Jewar
articles.description: Noida International Airport commenced commercial operations today with an IndiGo flight from Lucknow, marking a significant achievement for the greenfield project. Farmers who provided land for the airport were among the inaugural passengers. The airport aims to become a major aviation and cargo hub, fostering economic growth and easing traffic at Delhi's IGI Airport.

articles.title: Another start, another miss: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi falls for 21 against Sri Lanka A
articles.description: Teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi once again failed to convert a promising start into a substantial score for India A.  After racing to 21 off 14 balls against Sri Lanka A, he was caught at point. This follows similar dismissals in previous matches, highlighting a recurring pattern of quick starts but a lack of significant conversion.

articles.title: Tear gas, burning Tesla and smashed windows: 20,000 protest G7 summit in Geneva ahead of Trump's arrival
articles.description: Geneva witnessed clashes as thousands protested the G7 summit, with police using tear gas against stone-throwing demonstrators. While most of the 20,000 marchers remained peaceful, a "Black Bloc" of militants caused damage, including setting a car ablaze and smashing bank windows. Organizers had prepared extensively, but violence erupted, leading to arrests and heightened security for the upcoming summit in France.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal lifts markets: Asian stocks soar, dollar slides to June 5 low, oil tumbles & gold jumps 2%
articles.description: Global markets surged as the US and Iran announced a framework agreement to end their conflict, leading to hopes of lower energy prices and easing inflation. The Strait of Hormuz reopened, causing oil prices to tumble and boosting investor appetite for riskier assets. Asian equities rallied significantly, while the US dollar weakened against major currencies.

articles.title: AP Inter Supplementary Results 2026 expected soon: BIEAP to release scorecards at bie.ap.gov.in; check steps here
articles.description: Board of Intermediate Education, Andhra Pradesh (BIEAP) is expected to release the AP Inter Supplementary Results 2026, though the exact date has not been officially confirmed. The advanced supplementary examinations were conducted from May 21 to June 4, while the practical exams concluded on June 11. These exams provide students a second opportunity to clear failed subjects or improve their scores, with a minimum of 35 percent required in each subject to pass. Once released, students will be able to access their results on the official BIEAP portals, as well as through Manabadi, DigiLocker, and WhatsApp services using their hall ticket number and date of birth.

articles.title: MHT CET PCM Result 2026: Scorecards for engineering aspirants soon at cetcell.mahacet.org
articles.description: MHT CET PCM Result 2026: MHT CET 2026 PCM result is expected to be released soon by the State Common Entrance Test Cell, Maharashtra, on cetcell.mahacet.org. Lakhs of engineering aspirants can download their scorecards using application credentials. The exam saw over 7.25 lakh registrations with percentile-based normalization across shifts. PCB results were declared earlier on June 8. CAP counselling is likely to begin in late June after result publication and merit preparation for admissions across engineering colleges in Maharashtra state.

articles.title: Indian railways recruits 43,781 candidates in 2025-26, expands tech driven hiring and annual exam calendar system
articles.description: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw reviewed the Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) process, stressing transparency, accuracy, and faster, technology-driven hiring. Indian Railways recruited 43,781 candidates in FY 2025–26 across key categories including ALPs, technicians, and paramedical staff. CBT-based exams, expanded tablet testing, and a multilingual system were highlighted. The minister also noted strong candidate response to the annual recruitment calendar and directed improved communication and misinformation control to strengthen trust in the recruitment system nationwide.

articles.title: UDF launches legal offensive against Pranit More and show organisers, moves NHRC over cadaver-related dignity concerns
articles.description: The United Doctors Front (UDF) has launched legal action against comedian Pranit More and comedy show organisers, issuing a legal notice and filing a petition before the NHRC seeking suo motu cognizance of broader human rights concerns. UDF alleges that certain content undermines the dignity of women, deceased persons, and the medical profession while potentially discouraging body donation. The organisation has called for accountability from digital platforms and urged safeguards against the commercialisation of content that harms human dignity.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Oliver Tree
articles.description: In a devastating turn of events, the music industry is in shock after the untimely death of Oliver Tree, the 32-year-old singer-songwriter who lost his life in a helicopter accident in Brazil. Renowned for his eclectic electro-pop sound and quirky image, Oliver's song 'Life Goes On' now carries a heartbreaking new depth.

articles.title: Oliver Tree passes away: Stars pay tribute
articles.description: The music world mourns Oliver Tree's sudden death at 32 in a tragic helicopter crash over Rio de Janeiro. Friends like Melanie Martinez, Bebe Rexha, Kid Cudi, and T-Pain have shared heartfelt tributes, remembering his dedication, talent, and infectious joy. His untimely passing has left many in shock, highlighting the fragility of life.

articles.title: 'I'm Game' poster: Dulquer Salmaan's stylish look wins praise
articles.description: Dulquer Salmaan's grand comeback to Malayalam cinema is imminent with 'I'm Game', set for an August 20th Onam release. The poster, showcasing DQ in a sharp white suit, has ignited online buzz. Directed by Nahas Hidhayath, the film boasts a strong supporting cast including Antony Varghese Pepe and Mysskin in his acting debut.
